meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome tutorial hell. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break out of tutorial hell. Learn to build production-ready SaaS apps using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led, advanced-level roadmap for students.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start architecting. By utilizing a SaaS starter kit, you unify authentication, database schemas, and billing logic into a repeatable template. Paired with Antigravity and Claude Code, you shift from passive learner to active builder, shipping production-grade code in record time.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'To-Do List' tutorial, yet your GitHub remains empty. You are trapped in the cycle of endless consumption, building projects that never see the light of production. It is a subtle, soul-crushing stagnation that separates the dreamers from the engineers.

Today, we break the loop. By adopting a 'SaaS Starter Kit' architecture, you stop reinventing the auth wheel and start focusing on unique value. We will leverage Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent, iterative development, transforming your workflow from tutorial follower to SaaS architect.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Blueprint
steps.description: Establish the core architecture, including database schema, user roles, and subscription logic before writing a single line of application code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level SaaS architecture document for a multi-tenant application. Include Prisma schema for User, Subscription, and Project entities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate a project structure for a Next.js SaaS using Prisma and NextAuth. Focus on a clean, scalable folder hierarchy.'

steps.title: Bootstrap with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate code based on your defined schema, ensuring type safety and consistency across the stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a full-stack starter kit based on the previous schema. Include authentication hooks, protected API routes, and a Stripe checkout flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'npm install @antigravity/core && npx antigravity bootstrap --template saas-standard'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Auth
steps.description: Integrate authentication that handles session management and role-based access control out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure NextAuth.js with GitHub and Google providers. Create a middleware that restricts access to /dashboard to authenticated users only.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Update middleware.ts to enforce auth checks and redirect unauthorized users to the sign-in page.'

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Sync your local database with your production schema using automated migration scripts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a migration script that initializes the database with default billing plans and initial user roles.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Shipping
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build out your unique value proposition, one feature at a time, using the starter kit as a foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current codebase and suggest the most efficient way to add a 'Usage Metering' feature for the current SaaS subscription model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /fix 'Implement the usage metering logic in the API route handlers and ensure the database updates on every request.'

pro_tips: Never hardcode environment variables; always use a centralized 'env.mjs' validator to catch configuration errors at build time.
Treat your starter kit as a product, not a project; version it in a private repository so you can pull updates as your skills evolve.
Focus on the 'Happy Path' first: get a user from registration to a paid subscription before styling a single CSS element.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your auth logic; bugs in security are the only ones you cannot afford to ship.
Don't build your own billing; integrate Stripe's customer portal directly to handle subscription lifecycle management automatically.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is functional, deploy to Vercel or Railway. Then, find your first paying beta user to validate your product-market fit.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the technical roadmap to escape the tutorial trap. Stop building for practice and start building for production. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
